name: Madeira Roast Pheasant
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 450°F. Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a large roasting pan. Stir in onion, celery, carrots, bay leaves, and Herbs de Provence. Place pheasant giblets, necks, chicken wings, and backs on top of the vegetable mixture. Roast until well browned, turning frequently, about 40 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 375°F.
Meanwhile, make herb butter by mixing 1 cup butter with 1 tablespoon Herbs de Provence. Pat pheasants dry inside and out. Loosen breast skin on each by gently sliding fingers under the neck flap and down between the breast meat and skin, being careful not to tear the skin. Spread 1-1/2 tablespoons herb butter evenly over the breast under the skin of each. Sprinkle each cavity with salt and pepper. Place 1 tablespoon herb butter into each cavity. Truss pheasants to hold shape. Arrange on the sides atop the vegetable mixture in the roasting pan.
Roast for 15 minutes, basting frequently with pan juices. Turn pheasants to the second side and roast for another 15 minutes, basting frequently. Turn pheasants breast-side up. Add Madeira to the pan and continue roasting until juices run pale pink when the thickest part of the thigh is pierced, basting frequently, about 15 minutes. Transfer pheasants to a platter and discard string. Tent with foil.
Add broth to the roasting pan. Bring to a boil over low heat, scraping up any browned bits, until reduced to 3 cups liquid, about 6 minutes. Strain sauce and remove grease. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a small saucepan over low heat. Add flour and stir for 3 minutes. Whisk in the sauce in thin streams. Simmer for 5 minutes, stirring frequently. Adjust seasoning if needed. Pour into a bowl. Garnish pheasant with parsley. Pass sauce separately.
Available at specialty foods stores.
(Ouida & Vince Kelly) (C) 1987 Bon Appetit Publishing Corp. Compliments of Fred Peters
